seo.title: Building Code Generation with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building code generation agents with OpenAI Assistants API enables powerful LLM-driven development workflows with built-in sandbox execution and iterative refinement. However, the high-risk nature of generating and executing arbitrary code demands robust guardrails to prevent unsafe operations, credential leaks, and syntax errors. Vex provides comprehensive verification to ensure code safety, validity, and sandbox compliance before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with OpenAI Assistants API
architectureOverview.description:
The typical architecture starts with an Assistant configured with code interpreter and file search tools. User requests trigger a new Thread, and the Assistant generates code iteratively through Run cycles. Each code artifact is executed in OpenAI's sandbox, validated against tests, and refined based on execution feedback.

Run completion events trigger Vex webhooks for verification, checking code safety, syntax, dependency risks, and credential exposure. Verified code passes to deployment pipelines while failed verifications trigger Assistant refinement loops.

This closed-loop system maintains context across iterations while Vex ensures each code generation step meets security and quality standards.

risks.name: Code interpreter executing unsafe operations in sandbox
risks.description: Assistant may generate code with syscalls, network requests, or file system modifications that violate sandbox policies
risks.mitigation: Vex scans for dangerous APIs and enforces sandbox execution policies before code runs

risks.name: No credential leakage detection
risks.description: Generated code may embed API keys, passwords, or secrets from training data or context
risks.mitigation: Vex performs regex and semantic analysis to detect and redact credentials

risks.name: Thread context growing beyond model limits silently
risks.description: Long-running threads accumulate code artifacts exceeding token limits without warning
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors thread token usage and triggers context summarization

risks.name: Function call arguments hallucinated outside valid schema
risks.description: Code interpreter tool calls use invalid parameters breaking execution flow
risks.mitigation: Vex validates tool call JSON against OpenAPI schemas before execution

vexIntegration.title: Python Vex Webhook for Code Safety
vexIntegration.description: Verify code safety, syntax, and credential leakage on run completion

vexIntegration.title: TypeScript Vex Webhook Handler
vexIntegration.description: Express.js webhook endpoint for real-time Vex verification

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex Webhook with OpenAI
vexIntegration.description: Configure Assistant to trigger Vex verification on every run completion
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient
import openai

vex = VexClient(api_key='your-vex-key')
client = openai.OpenAI()

# Update assistant with webhook
assistant = client.beta.assistants.update(
    assistant_id='asst_your_id',
    tool_resources={
        'code_interpreter': {
            'file_ids': ['test_suite.py']
        }
    },
    # Note: Webhooks configured via OpenAI platform dashboard
    # Point 'Run Completed' events to your Vex endpoint
)

# Vex monitors all threads automatically once webhook active
verification_policy = vex.create_policy({
    'name': 'code-generation-guardrails',
    'rules': [
        'no_network_access',
        'no_file_writes',
        'no_eval_exec',
        'credential_free',
        'syntax_valid',
        'test_passing'
    ],
    'auto_block': True
})

print(f'Policy ID: {verification_policy.id} - Active for all code gen threads')
